The Israelites came to Jordan with faith, having been told that they should pass through it. In doing what is right, let us go as far as we can and depend on the Lord. Joshua led them. Special attention is given to his early rising, as was later in other situations, which shows how little he sought his own comfort. Those who want to accomplish great things must rise early. Do not love sleep, or you will come to poverty. All people in public positions should always attend to the duty of their place. The people were to follow the ark. We must walk according to the guidance of the word and the direction of the Spirit in everything; then peace will be upon us as it was on the Israel of God. But we must follow our ministers only as they follow Christ. Their entire journey through the wilderness was an untrodden path, but this one through Jordan even more so. While we are here, we must expect and prepare to pass through ways that we have not passed before; but in the path of duty we may proceed with boldness and cheerfulness. Whether we are called to suffer poverty, pain, labor, persecution, reproach, or death, we are following the Author and Finisher of our faith; nor can we set our feet in any dangerous or difficult place throughout our journey, but faith will see there the prints of the Redeemer’s feet, who trod that very path to glory above, and bids us follow him, so that where he is, we may be also. They were to make themselves holy. If we want to experience the effects of God's love and power, we must put away sin and be careful not to grieve the Holy Spirit of God.

The waters of Jordan will be cut off. This must happen in a way that has never happened before, except for when the Red Sea was divided. That miracle is repeated; God has the same power to finish the salvation of his people as he does to begin it; the WORD of the Lord was as truly with Joshua as with Moses. God's appearances for his people should encourage faith and hope. God’s work is perfect, he will keep his people. Jordan’s flood cannot keep out Israel, Canaan’s force cannot drive them out again.

Jordan overflowed all its banks. This magnified the power of God and his kindness to Israel. Although those who oppose the salvation of God's people have every advantage, yet God can and will conquer. This passage over Jordan, as an entrance to Canaan after their long, weary wanderings in the wilderness, showed what the believer’s passage through death to heaven would be like, after he has finished his wanderings in this sinful world. Jesus, typified by the ark, has gone before, and he crossed the river when it flooded the country most severely. Let us treasure up experiences of His faithful and tender care, so that they may help our faith and hope in the final conflict.
